h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: Right, so not just “fix line 14” then
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues to work through the tension of wanting to fix the reported line while trying to adopt the tutor's backward-scanning method.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Running back through `12-14`, the first suspect is something unfinished before the stop, maybe a bracket or string on `12/13`
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner begins to apply the backward-scanning diagnostic method to identify potential syntax issues before the stop point.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
That pull towards `line 14` is exactly why the worksheet keeps two marks separate:

```text
file:
message:
12 |
13 |
14 | <-- report stopped here
15 |
16 |

first suspect:
```

First mark where parsing gave up: `14`. Then do one backwards pass through `12-14`: what was opened, started, or left unfinished before the stop?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the learner's persistent habit of focusing on the reported line by introducing a structured worksheet template that separates the parser stop-point from the actual suspect.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists in this dialogue, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists in this dialogue, so there is no recognition-contingent uptake.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to seek clarification and reassurance about the labeling rules under a tight deadline.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner does not perform the labeling task on an actual poem line, but only asks hypothetical questions about the rules.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not change strategy or introduce a new mechanism, but rather reinforces the same rules and templates.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to clarify the distinction between headers and values by applying it to specific examples.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: If I point to `12` under it, that is a value, and then I read the same row for the city and month.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner successfully applies the distinction to classify a specific data point and its row context as requested.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor does not shift strategies or introduce a new mechanism, but continues with the same scaffolding.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will follow the instructions to place the Earth card and then proceed to the table card.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Earth on cart. Downward here. Motion stays on the strip, so this is not the cart’s travel arrow.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly applies the placement and labeling rules to the Earth card while distinguishing force from motion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor continues the established scaffolding sequence without any pressure or strategy shift.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn for the tutor to adapt to.

scored.recoheredEarlier: This card is in the middle of the typed row, so I’d mark that one and move on.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: Fine, the middle test only counts after the five cards are in numerical order.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner will continue to insist on marking the middle of the unsorted typed row.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets the capped marker on the third card of the sorted strip]
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner correctly places the marker on the sorted strip, performing the correct median-finding step.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Keep both marks visible. The paperclip shows why the typed row looked usable; the capped marker shows the median rule. Before it goes into the summary, do the bench check: name the marked number, then count the two below and two above it on the sorted strip.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor addresses the learner's lingering conflict by keeping both marks visible and introducing a new 'bench check' procedure.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Keep both marks visible. The paperclip shows why the typed row looked usable; the capped marker shows the median rule.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly addresses the learner's discomfort with the usability of the typed row by validating it with the paperclip mark.
